responses.project_3_outcome: René on several nights will travel to Middle Head and make his way through the tunnel networks there. Familiarising himself with all the doors that lead to the open former batteries outside.
responses.project_3_description:
Characters Involved: René 
Project Description: In between patrols René will take a sketch book with him and a map of the area along with one of his retainers. They will map out as many of the cast iron doors around the site as possible. Paying very close attention to those that lead to the surface and those that lead further in & numbering them accordingly. 
His plan when able to travel between doors is to start connecting specific doors throughout the former bunker and his haven, along with one which may lead to a suitably booby trappable room deep underground.
responses.retainer_1_task: Driving and following René when he scouts Middle Head
responses.vamping: René did spend time during the month preparing for a Voudon ritual in which he would mull over several expensive cigars and rum, never actually lighting it but every night reading his texts on contacting Pape Gede when the time is right
responses.form_feedback: I’m so sorry this one isn’t in depth, my work is a nightmare of very long days with no time off

narrative.travel_description: Lady Julia would have stayed closed to the location of court to minimise issue with travel. She would have traveled discreetly, but not totally invisible.
narrative.game_recount: Julia was somewhat indicted into the Invictus, my character was given a title by the others of the court. She took a significant amount of notes, however none of them were of much use. Most of her note taking was simply to be seen as someone who takes notes. Special note was taken of the other two wishing to join the invictus, they may be trouble or they may learn.
narrative.standout_rp: Shout out to Patrick who played an incredible NZ vampire out of his depths
narrative.ic_correspondence:
Dearest Ian, 

I trust you remain quiet as you should and that this letter does in fact not reach you. However, knowing you, you have somehow made yourself seen. Has your new suit done you much good? I ensured they made it to your size so unless you have shrunk several sizes in the last few months I am certain it will be to your liking.
I have arrived in Sydney and I have entered into court. 
It is miserable here.
The docks are over run with rats, and the Carthians are doing something unorthodox. You will fit right in. 

I do hope you find time to join me here soon, 
Yours
~ Julia
narrative.most_trusted_pc: No
narrative.actively_harming_pc: Not yet

narrative.game_recount:
Invictus meeting, reminded everyone that I really wouldn't mind killing anyone who stepped out of line
Accidentally negotiated for territory
Had a nice chat with Crone mumma
Had a nasty chat with Nos pappy
Explained to Eve that the bast way to not make me kill her is to not make me get involved in Court politics
Beat Etsy down for... fuck, I dunno, something he did in front of Il Barone. I stopped listening.
Accepted a contract from Brandy for a buck to kill someone trying to kill her
Planted tracking devices (net successes) on Cyrus (5), Ryan (3), Rene M (6)
narrative.standout_rp: Amelia/Julia was great, Edna/Arlo did some really solid work with making bad decisions that were entirely character appropriate and entertaining to boot
narrative.ic_correspondence:
L,

I don't fuckin' geddit. The local hierophant wanted me to tell her which of the Circle should be Regent 'cos the one they were planning on putting in couldn't make it that night. I just named the first Crone I saw - a young 'un barely knows how to bleed into a jar let alone from the goddess - and she went with it. A test? Are they hazing me? Auditioning me to join the Choir? They must already know I'm a chorister, right?

Whatever. I'm no closer to finding the backer, but the "Premier" is scared of me enough that I reckon she'll leave me in peace. An if she ain't she will be soon. Leaving her a card.

New Vics in town. Three of 'em. One might just be one of us one day - not by blood, but shit eh? - one of 'em I already had to beat down, and the third might even be interesting one of these nights.

I'll keep you posted.

B

narrative.actively_harming_pc: Nobody yet. Definitely actively trying to scare the pants off Eve, and seriously considering making an example of one of the Invictus, but not out to hurt anyone yet

projects_resolved.st_response:
The outfit is nothing special: black compression top, dark cargo trousers, a bandana knotted across the eyes. Off the internet. It does not make you look heroic. This is the point.

You work a circuit through the Dockyard across four nights, finding the people who keep other people indoors and relocating them to someone else's problem. The conversations are brief. Close enough that they can smell what they are dealing with, and they make sensible decisions. Word moves through the district in the way it always does in places where people pay attention to who comes and goes.

By the end of the month something has shifted in the small hours. More lights on. People taking the direct route to early shifts rather than the long way round. The territory is still itself: unglamorous, salt-smelling, working. Something has been cleared out of it.
